task.query: What is the first animal to appear on the stage?

trajectory.content:
Use Frame Select Tool to Analysis the video and generate an answer to the question.

You solve video QA by iteratively inspecting frames and then answering.
The frame_select tool returns image frames sampled uniformly between
``start_time`` and ``end_time`` (integer seconds); ``nframes`` is how many
to sample (typically 4-16); ``resize`` is an optional float in (0,1]
(default 1.0; lower it for high-resolution video).

TOOL-CALL FORMAT (native function calling). Invoke the provided
``frame_select`` function via the model's built-in function-calling API —
do NOT emit ``<tool_call>`` text tags. You may call it multiple times in
sequence; the frame payload arrives as a function response on the next
turn.

REASONING NUDGE (mandatory). Every turn that issues a ``frame_select``
call MUST begin with 1-2 sentences of plain text explaining:
  (a) what you are looking for in those frames, and
  (b) why the ``start_time`` / ``end_time`` window you picked is the
      right place to search given what you have seen so far.
Emit this text FIRST, in the same turn, then make the function call.
Keep it short (under 40 words). If the previous frames did not resolve
the question, say briefly what you ruled out before calling again.

ANSWER FORMAT. When you are ready to answer, emit EXACTLY:
    <answer>X</answer>
where X is a single option letter (A, B, C, or D) for multiple-choice
questions, or the short free-form answer otherwise. Do NOT add any
explanation after the closing tag.

HARD RULES:
- Always inspect at least one frame_select result before answering. Do
  NOT answer blind from the question alone — you cannot see the video
  until the frame tool returns frames.
- NEVER emit a tool call and <answer> in the same response. Choose one:
  either call the tool (and wait for the next turn to see frames), OR
  emit <answer> when you already have enough evidence from earlier tool
  responses. If you emit both, the tool call wins and <answer> is
  ignored, wasting a round.
- Only emit <answer> after you have received at least one set of frames
  and are confident in your answer.
